description: Natural-language-to-SQL on an Oracle Autonomous Database using Select AI (DBMS_CLOUD_AI). Two-tool pair: SelectAIProvisionTool sets up the NL2SQL profile once on the target ADB, NL2SQLTool turns user questions into SQL and runs them on every agent turn. This is NL2SQL ONLY — for plain SQL execution against an Oracle catalog, use AIDP's first-party SQL Tool (catalogType=EXTERNAL).

tools.description:
Provisions an Oracle ADB Select AI NL2SQL profile + agent tool so the companion NL2SQLTool can answer natural-language questions about a fixed set of tables. Runs DBMS_CLOUD_AI.CREATE_PROFILE + DBMS_CLOUD_AI_AGENT.CREATE_TOOL, records the result in an AIDP_NL2SQL_PROFILES audit table the tool auto-creates on first run, and is fully idempotent via a config-hash check.

When to use: Run this ONCE per (connection, schema, table set, model) combination, BEFORE the agent's first NL2SQL turn. Run again ONLY when the user changes the target tables/schema/model/credential — repeat calls with the same inputs are safe (skipped via hash). DO NOT use this for ad-hoc plain-SQL execution; for that, use AIDP's first-party SQL Tool (catalogType=EXTERNAL).

How to discover inputs: For `aidp_catalog_key`, call CatalogBrowserTool(list='catalogs') and ask the user which AIDP catalog binding exposes the target ADB. For `target_schema` + `target_tables`, ask the user — never default `target_tables` to a wildcard for schemas with many tables (Select AI prompts get huge fast; 8-20 tables is the sweet spot).

How to communicate: Report the action (`created` / `unchanged` / `recreated`) and the profile name in one sentence. On `unchanged`, tell the user the agent can proceed straight to NL2SQLTool with no further setup.

Example: SelectAIProvisionTool(aidp_catalog_key='construction_catalog.construction_schema.construction_db', profile_name='constr_demo', target_schema='ADMIN', target_tables='INCIDENT_REPORTS,WORK_ORDERS,EMPLOYEES') -> NL2SQLTool(profile_name='constr_demo', action='RUNSQL', prompt='how many open incidents last month?').

schema.name: profile_name
schema.description: Oracle identifier (<=120 chars) that names the Select AI profile + agent tool. Reused as the profile_name argument on every NL2SQLTool call. Example: `constr_demo`.

schema.name: target_schema
schema.description: Uppercase Oracle schema name that owns the listed tables. Required. Example: `ADMIN`.

schema.name: target_tables
schema.description: Comma-separated table names within target_schema. Order-insensitive (the tool sorts before hashing). Keep this list focused — Select AI accuracy degrades sharply past ~30 tables in a single profile. Example: `INCIDENT_REPORTS,WORK_ORDERS,EMPLOYEES`.

schema.name: comments
schema.description: Optional free-text description of the schema/tables fed to the LLM as a hint (helps accuracy). Example: `Construction site incident + work-order tables; PROJECT_ID joins to PROJECTS.PROJECT_ID`.
examples: Sales tables; ORDERS joins to CUSTOMERS on CUSTOMER_ID.

schema.name: force_recreate
schema.description: When true, drop and recreate the profile even if the config hash matches. Use only if you suspect the on-ADB state is corrupted.

credential_name.help: Display name of a SECRET_TOKEN credential in AIDPs Credential Store. Leave empty to fall back to resource_principal (which 401s against public AIDP data-plane endpoints per the Jun-17 thread).

aidp_credential_name.help: Display name of a SECRET_TOKEN credential in AIDPs Credential Store holding DB connect info (username, password, connection_string, wallet_b64). Distinct from the in-ADB credential_name above which is a DBMS_CLOUD credential.

_confDescriptions.aidp_catalog_key: Preferred: an AIDP catalog binding (catalog.schema.dbName) that exposes ADB credentials via get_connection_data. Find via AIDP Console → Catalogs. Leave blank if using the explicit conn_string/username/password/wallet fields.
_confDescriptions.conn_string: Fallback ADB connection string (TNS alias or full descriptor). Only use when aidp_catalog_key is blank. Example: `myadb_high`.
_confDescriptions.username: Fallback DB user. Only use when aidp_catalog_key is blank. Example: `ADMIN`.
_confDescriptions.password: (secret) Fallback DB password. Only use when aidp_catalog_key is blank.
_confDescriptions.wallet_b64: (secret) Base64-encoded wallet zip for the ADB. Only use when aidp_catalog_key is blank.
_confDescriptions.wallet_password: (secret) Wallet password (if the wallet is encrypted).
_confDescriptions.llm_model_id: OCI GenAI model id used by Select AI. Default `xai.grok-4`. Override only when the user explicitly asks. Catalog: .aidp/knowledge/oci-genai-models.md.
_confDescriptions.model_provider: Select AI provider. Currently only `oci` is supported by this toolkit.
_confDescriptions.credential_name: Oracle DB credential used by Select AI to call OCI GenAI. Default `OCI$RESOURCE_PRINCIPAL` (auto-created by `ENABLE_RESOURCE_PRINCIPAL`).
_confDescriptions.compartment_id: OCI compartment OCID for GenAI inference. OCI Console → Identity & Security → Compartments.
_confDescriptions.auth_mode: OCI auth flavor for any ADB-adjacent OCI calls. `resource_principal` is the default (works inside AIDP compute).
_confDescriptions.fingerprint: OCI API key fingerprint. Auto-filled from ~/.oci/config.
_confDescriptions.private_key_content: (secret) PEM private key — only needed when auth_mode=user_principal.

tools.description:
Answers natural-language questions about Oracle ADB tables by calling DBMS_CLOUD_AI.GENERATE against a Select AI profile that SelectAIProvisionTool created. Returns rows (RUNSQL), just the generated SQL (SHOWSQL), a natural-language summary (NARRATE), or annotated SQL (EXPLAINSQL).

When to use: Use on every agent turn where the user asks a question about data in the ADB tables registered with the named profile. Default `action` is `RUNSQL`. For plain SQL execution where the user wrote the SQL themselves, use AIDP's first-party SQL Tool — this tool is for natural language.

How to discover inputs: `profile_name` comes from the SelectAIProvisionTool result. If the agent gets a 'profile not found' or ORA-20000-class error, call SelectAIProvisionTool FIRST then retry. For `prompt`, pass the user's question verbatim. For `action`, default to RUNSQL; pick NARRATE when the user wants a sentence answer instead of a table, SHOWSQL when the user is debugging the generated SQL, EXPLAINSQL when they want annotated SQL.

How to communicate: For RUNSQL, summarize row count + first 3 rows; for NARRATE, return the answer directly; for SHOWSQL/EXPLAINSQL, format the SQL in a code block. Read-only by construction: this tool refuses to execute generated SQL whose first keyword isn't SELECT or WITH, even if DBMS_CLOUD_AI returns DML — caller will see a `fail` envelope with a clear message, never a silent data mutation.

Example: NL2SQLTool(profile_name='constr_demo', action='RUNSQL', prompt='how many open incidents last month?', max_rows=100) → {ok: true, data: {sql: 'SELECT count(*) ...', rows: [{COUNT: 47}], rows_fetched: 1}}.

schema.name: profile_name
schema.description: Name of the Select AI profile created by SelectAIProvisionTool. Required. Example: `constr_demo`.

schema.name: prompt
schema.description: The user's natural-language question. Pass verbatim — DBMS_CLOUD_AI converts it to SQL using the profile's table list. Example: `how many open incidents last month?`.
schema.default: how many open incidents last month?
examples: how many open incidents last month?
top 5 employees by hours worked this quarter
sum of work-order costs grouped by project

schema.name: action
schema.description: Which DBMS_CLOUD_AI action to invoke. `RUNSQL` runs the generated SQL and returns rows. `SHOWSQL` returns the SQL string only. `NARRATE` returns a natural-language summary. `EXPLAINSQL` returns annotated SQL. Default `RUNSQL`.

schema.name: max_rows
schema.description: Cap on rows fetched when action=RUNSQL. Default 100, hard ceiling 10000. Larger values can blow the agent's response budget.

aidp_credential_name.help: Display name of a SECRET_TOKEN credential in AIDPs Credential Store holding DB connect info (username, password, connection_string, wallet_b64). Distinct from the in-ADB credential_name above which is a DBMS_CLOUD credential.

_confDescriptions.aidp_catalog_key: Preferred ADB connection source. AIDP Console → Catalogs. Leave blank if using explicit conn_string/username/password/wallet.
_confDescriptions.conn_string: Fallback TNS alias or descriptor. Only use when aidp_catalog_key is blank.
_confDescriptions.username: Fallback DB user. Only use when aidp_catalog_key is blank.
_confDescriptions.default_action: Action used when the runtime param `action` is blank. Default RUNSQL.
_confDescriptions.max_rows: Default row cap for RUNSQL; runtime param overrides.
_confDescriptions.safety_check: When true, run SHOWSQL first and reject anything that isn't SELECT/WITH. Always recommended; turn off only for diagnostic builds.
_confDescriptions.auth_mode: OCI auth flavor for any OCI calls. `resource_principal` works inside AIDP compute.
_confDescriptions.fingerprint: OCI API key fingerprint. Auto-filled from ~/.oci/config.
_confDescriptions.private_key_content: (secret) PEM private key — only needed when auth_mode=user_principal.
